items.title: Does Pauline Hanson's claim of growing support in the ACT pass the pub test?

children.content: The conventional wisdom is that Canberra is a largely left-leaning city, but the national capital is now among places in One Nation's sights as support for the party surges across the country.

items.title: Hikers lost near Kosciuszko found by artificial intelligence drone

children.content: Rescuers were able to locate the hikers less than five hours after they were reported missing.

items.title: Murder trial mementos: The Chamberlain artefacts too sensitive to exhibit

children.content: Forty years ago, the chance discovery of a bloodied matinee jacket unravelled a murder conviction. Now, that evidence lives inside a national museum's collection that's too sensitive for public display.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a couple outside court, they are holding up a large photo of Lindy Chamberlain holding baby Azaria.

items.title: 'Systemic issue': Abuse sees Canberra referees quitting after a year

children.content: Canberra soccer match officials say referee abuse is a "systemic issue" across all sporting codes, with increasingly hostile behaviour from spectators, players and coaches causing 44 per cent of Capital Football's referees to leave their roles after a year.

items.title: 'Everybody is equal at the table': Inside the ACT's Circle Sentencing court

children.content: A pilot program allowing First Nations offenders to be sentenced by magistrates sitting alongside elders in the ACT Supreme Court secures funding for another three years.

items.title: How a discarded coffee cup helped solve a 24-year cold case killing

children.content: Twenty-four years after the killing of a Canberra grandmother, a coffee cup discarded on an east coast road trip leads to the arrest and convictions of her killers.

items.title: 'Insurance policy' hoping to protect quolls if bird flu arrives in ACT

children.content: A population of endangered eastern quolls is being protected by a sanctuary in Canberra, but the threat of bird flu could mean that protection does not go far enough.

items.title: The covert narcissist: Charming on the surface, destructive beneath

children.content: As "narcissist" becomes the catch-all insult of the social media age, here is the crucial difference between everyday selfishness and a serious personality disorder.

items.title: Labor unveils new powers to strengthen social media ban

children.content: Six months into Australia's world-first social media ban for under-16s, the federal government has announced tougher laws that it says will hold big tech to account and keep kids safe online.

items.title: How many products in your daily life are made from fossil fuels?

children.content: Coal, oil and gas are major ingredients for far more than just energy. Why are we so reliant on oil, and how difficult would it be to find other resources?

items.title: Panthers coach gives 13-word press conference after consecutive losses

items.title: While teaching about pancreatic cancer, John realised he ticked every box

children.content: Clinical worker John McNeur didn't realise he was unwell until he was delivering a health workshop on pancreatic cancer in the remote Northern Territory — and he began ticking all of the boxes. Now he's on a mission to raise awareness.

items.title: The dry heart of Australia is now awash with water

children.content: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.
